title: vicia sativa var. peregrina
intro: Vicia sativa var. peregrina, also known as the wandering pea or creeping vetch, is a variety of the common vetch plant that belongs to the legume family. It is a perennial plant with climbing stems and compound leaves that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Vicia sativa var. peregrina include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion, making it a popular herb in herbal remedies. Vicia sativa var. peregrina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from respiratory problems to skin conditions.
